metadata.name: Stainless Steel 316
metadata.title: Stainless Steel 316 Settings
metadata.description: Before laser cleaning, Stainless Steel 316 surfaces require careful parameter selection After cleaning, Stainless Steel 316 surfaces show restored appearance with controlled parameters

author.name: Yi-Chun Lin
author.title: Ph.D.
credentials: Ph.D. Materials Engineering, National Taiwan University, 2018
Post-Ph.D. fellowship at TSMC's laser fab lab, 2018-2020
Assisted in projects on ultrafast laser applications
author.imageAlt: Yi-Chun Lin, Ph.D., Laser Processing Engineer at National Taiwan University, in lab setting

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for metal oxide removal

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal metal absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning resolution

energyDensity.description: Energy per unit area for oxide removal

pulseWidth.description: Pulse duration for minimal heat-affected zone

scanSpeed.description: Beam travel speed for uniform treatment

passCount.description: Number of cleaning cycles for complete removal

power.description: Typical range for industrial fiber lasers cleaning metals like SS316; starting at 100 W avoids overheating due to SS316's reflectivity (~65% at 1064 nm); max limited to prevent melting (threshold ~500-1000 W depending on pulse); source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and Anisimov et al. (2018) in Optics Express.

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contamination removal; slower (100-1000 mm/s) for heavy rust/oil, faster (2000-5000 mm/s) for light contaminants; 2000 mm/s optimal for SS316 surface prep balancing efficiency and minimal HAZ; source: Laserax equipment manuals and industry standards from SPIE proceedings (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime standard for industrial cleaning to ablate contaminants without deep substrate penetration; 50 ns recommended for SS316 to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm) given its thermal conductivity (16 W/m·K); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precision but costlier; source: Laser physics studies by Kunze et al. (2019) in Applied Surface Science.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; 20-50 kHz for SS316 to control heat accumulation (high freq increases temp rise); 50 kHz balances speed and safety; trade-off: higher rates speed up process but risk thermal damage; source: Academic literature, e.g., Pou et al. (2011) in Journal of Cleaner Production.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe ablation of contaminants on metals; SS316 damage threshold ~1.5-2.5 J/cm² for ns pulses at 1064 nm; 0.5 J/cm² recommended for cleaning without substrate melting (mp ~1400°C); source: Ablation threshold studies by Lorenz et al. (2017) in Applied Physics A.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for focused cleaning; smaller (20-50 μm) for fine details, larger (100-200 μm) for broad areas; 100 μm recommended for uniform SS316 surface prep; source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf lasers, per industry case studies.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy layers; 2 passes typical for complete SS316 cleaning without excessive wear; source: Industry practice from case studies in Metal Finishing Journal (2021) and manufacturer guidelines.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 30-50% for speed, 60-80% for thoroughness on reflective SS316; 60% recommended to avoid gaps while minimizing passes; source: Process optimization studies by Zhou et al. (2020) in Optics and Lasers in Engineering.
